diagrams.title: Linear Search Process Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how Linear Search examines each element sequentially from index 0 until target is found or array ends
diagrams.purpose: Understand sequential checking and how target value '67' is found at index 3 after 4 comparisons

diagrams.title: Best Case vs Worst Case Comparison
diagrams.description: Illustrates the difference between finding element at first position (best case) vs last position (worst case)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the range of possible outcomes in Linear Search and understand why time complexity is expressed in Big-O terms

diagrams.title: Linear Search vs Binary Search Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing why Binary Search is faster for sorted arrays
elements: Linear Search: Check each element sequentially
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate efficiency gain of Binary Search over Linear Search for sorted data
